title: T1000-E epoch persistence: settings/NVS not advancing the epoch (stuck), likely stale storage partition
description:
Enabled CONFIG_LICHEN_LINK_EPOCH_PERSIST (+SETTINGS/NVS) on the T1000-E puck with config IDENTICAL to the T-Echo (which validated clean at 0 replays, bd 3uhb). But the gateway-side reboot test shows a reproducible, DETERMINISTIC pattern: 0,0,1,1,1,1,1,1 replay-rejections across 8 T1000-E reboots (2 runs identical). That signature = exactly 1 replay per reboot from reboot 3 = the epoch is NOT advancing (stuck), not the random [128,255] coin-flip (which would be a mixed ~50% pattern). epoch_persist.c falls back to boot_epoch=1 every boot when settings_subsys_init()/settings_load_subtree() fails, which matches: gateway remembers (1,K), each reboot resends (1,0..) -> the low seq replays once against the bitmap, then recovers.

Root cause hypothesis: the T1000-E's storage_partition@0xbf000 (0x4000, same size as the T-Echo's @0xe4000) holds STALE non-LICHEN data - the T1000-E previously ran Meshtastic, whose NVS lives in that region. A settings-NVS mount over a partition with foreign data can fail or misbehave, so the epoch never persists. The T-Echo's storage was evidently clean.

FIX: full chip erase to clear the storage partition, then reflash - needs physical UF2-bootloader access (double-tap reset -> cp UF2), NOT possible over SMP OTA. After erase, re-run the gateway-side reboot test (scratchpad/epoch_t1000e_gw.py) expecting 0 replays like the T-Echo. Alternatively add CONFIG_MCUMGR_GRP_SETTINGS + an h_get on the epoch handler to read the persisted value over SMP and confirm it's stuck at 1.

IMPACT: minor. The T1000-E still completes CoAP round trips (coap.ok climbs after reboots); the epoch=1 fallback recovers after 1 frame, which is more robust than the random-epoch 50%-total-failure coin-flip, so the config is kept. Reference session 011WNEwnjQpVRfApJFak8Cpd.
